docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Bagus Wijanarko

LiDAR Quality Assurance Engineer

[Life-long learner | Geodata Engineering | Remote Sensing | R]

docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
[Replace this paragraph with your own bio. Write 3–4 sentences covering: your background and what you specialize in, the kinds of problems you work on, the tools and methods you use, and what you are currently looking for. Example below:]

I'm a Geodetic Engineer with interest in Geospatial Data Science, Remote Sensing and Applied GIS/Photogrammetry, currently learning R Language and Data Engineering to improve my role as LiDAR Quality Assurance Engineer. I'm very passionate at developing geospatial process automation, LiDAR explanatory data analysis, and discovering new insights of it to ensure the geospatial information quality derrived from it.

View My Projects Download CV

docs.title: Skills
docs.text:
• GIS & Remote Sensing

    • QGIS, ArcGIS Pro, Google Earth Engine
    • GDAL / OGR, GRASS GIS
    • Multispectral and SAR image analysis
    • Cloud Native Geospatial (COG, STAC, Zarr)

  • Programming

    • Python — GeoPandas, NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib
    • R — sf, terra, ggplot2
    • JavaScript — Leaflet, MapLibre GL
    • SQL, PostgreSQL + PostGIS

  • Machine Learning & GeoAI

    • Supervised classification — Random Forest, XGBoost
    • Deep learning for image segmentation — U-Net, SAM
    • scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow
    • Object detection in satellite imagery

  • Web Mapping & Data

    • Leaflet.js, Folium, MapLibre GL JS
    • Cloud storage — AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage
    • Data formats — GeoTIFF, GeoParquet, NetCDF
    • Streamlit for data-driven web apps

  • Data & Cloud

    • PostgreSQL + PostGIS
    • Cloud storage: AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage
    • Data formats: GeoJSON, GeoTIFF, NetCDF, Zarr, GeoParquet

  • Drone / UAV Data Processing

    • Mission planning and flight operations
    • Photogrammetry: Agisoft Metashape, OpenDroneMap
    • Point cloud processing: CloudCompare, PDAL

Download CV

docs.title: LiDAR Quality Assurance Specialist — APRIL
docs.text:
June 2024 – Present | Pangkalan Kerinci, Indonesia

  • Created and maintained a Quality Assurance framework for LiDAR data operational inventory, covering post-acquisition to pre-production processes
  • Developed advanced automation scripts to improve agility and productivity of the LiDAR QA workflow
  • Maintained geospatial databases to ensure full traceability of all QA processes, and conducted hands-on training for new recruits

docs.title: Conservation Area MRV Officer — Golden Agri Resources
docs.text:
May 2023 – May 2024 | Jakarta, Indonesia

  • Delineated supply base boundaries from third-party suppliers and investigated deforestation/peat development alerts raised by buyers and customers
  • Proactively engaged with suppliers and buyers to discuss confirmed active deforestation cases with the GAR team and relevant stakeholders
  • Assisted in spatial data creation and mining in support of European Union Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) compliance projects

docs.title: Traceability and Verification Officer — Golden Agri Resources
docs.text:
August 2022 – April 2023 | Jakarta, Indonesia

  • Conducted spatial verification of traceability declarations from third-party suppliers and delineated supply base maps into a geodatabase
  • Completed missing coordinate and address information in traceability declarations using public domain data
  • Maintained geodatabase completeness of attribute information related to the supply base

docs.title: Professional Fellowship Mentoring Participant — Esri Indonesia
docs.text:
February 2023 – April 2023 | Indonesia

  • Selected as one of few participants to develop a learning project in a two-month mentoring programme
  • Produced "Palm Oil Trace in the Atmosphere of Sumatra and Borneo," exploring GHG accumulation in palm oil supply chains using satellite sensor data
  • Applied ArcGIS products for geostatistics and data analytics in the context of sustainable palm oil

docs.title: Data Processor — Avirtech
docs.text:
December 2021 – July 2022 | Jakarta, Indonesia

  • Processed RGB and multispectral orthophoto data alongside GPS data for tree counting, estate mapping, terrain mapping, and geometric displacement quality control
  • Developed automation workflows for sugarcane weed detection and yellow palm detection using Principal Component Analysis (PCA) combined with Python (ArcPy)
  • Supported the mapping project management team in completing client projects end-to-end

docs.title: Research Intern — Indonesian National Institute of Aeronautics and Space (LAPAN)
docs.text:
January 2020 | Jakarta, Indonesia

  • Conducted land cover change analysis on Rote Island using object-based classification with Landsat and PlanetScope imagery in eCognition and ArcGIS
  • Identified land drought expansion (bare ground +21%) from 1999–2009 and agricultural area growth (vegetation +10%) from 2009–2019
  • Completed the full analysis within 20 working days

docs.title: Junior Project Assistant — Department of Geodetic Engineering, UGM
docs.text:
March 2019 – May 2019 | Yogyakarta, Indonesia

  • Designed canal blockages at three peatland locations using LiDAR elevation and intensity data for the Peat Restoration Agency (BRG)
  • Calculated the Normalized Burn Ratio (NBR) from Sentinel-2 data to support peat restoration planning
  • Estimated peat depth measurements representing 25% of total data, statistically sufficient using the Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) method in ArcGIS and Global Mapper

docs.title: Data Processor Assistant — KJSKB Rizarda dan Rekan
docs.text:
August 2018 | Jakarta Selatan, Indonesia

  • Assisted land parcel mapping for the government's Pendaftaran Tanah Sistematis Lengkap (PTSL) Jakarta Selatan project
  • Produced layouts for 31 survey drawings from two kelurahan (Bukit Duri and Manggarai Selatan) using AutoCAD Map 3D within 20 working days

docs.title: Bachelor's Degree in Geodetic Engineering
docs.text:
Universitas Gadjah Mada (UGM) | 2021

Focused on geospatial data science, remote sensing, applied GIS, and photogrammetry.

docs.title: Science Major (SMA)
docs.text: SMA Negeri 91 Jakarta | 2015

docs.title: Sample Notebook
docs.text:
In [1]: Copied! 
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import folium
import pandas as pd import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import folium In [ ]: Copied! 
# Placeholder time-series data
# Replace with: df = pd.read_csv("your_data.csv")
data = {
    "month": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun",
              "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"],
    "metric_a": [120, 135, 148, 162, 175, 190, 185, 178, 160, 145, 130, 118],
    "metric_b": [ 80,  88,  95, 105, 112, 120, 118, 110, 100,  92,  85,  78],
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)
df.head()
# Placeholder time-series data # Replace with: df = pd.read_csv("your_data.csv") data = { "month": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"], "metric_a": [120, 135, 148, 162, 175, 190, 185, 178, 160, 145, 130, 118], "metric_b": [ 80, 88, 95, 105, 112, 120, 118, 110, 100, 92, 85, 78], } df = pd.DataFrame(data) df.head() In [ ]: Copied! 
df[["metric_a", "metric_b"]].describe()
df[["metric_a", "metric_b"]].describe() In [4]: Copied! 
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))

ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_a"], marker="o", label="Metric A", color="steelblue")
ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_b"], marker="s", label="Metric B", color="coral")

ax.set_title("[YOUR CHART TITLE]", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
ax.set_xlabel("Month")
ax.set_ylabel("[Unit]")
ax.legend()
ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4)) ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_a"], marker="o", label="Metric A", color="steelblue") ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_b"], marker="s", label="Metric B", color="coral") ax.set_title("[YOUR CHART TITLE]", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold") ax.set_xlabel("Month") ax.set_ylabel("[Unit]") ax.legend() ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5) plt.tight_layout() plt.show() In [7]: Copied! 
# Placeholder point locations
# Replace with your own lat/lon data, e.g. from a GeoDataFrame or CSV
sites = pd.DataFrame({
    "name":  ["Site A", "Site B", "Site C", "Site D", "Site E"],
    "lat":   [28.61,    28.65,    28.58,    28.70,    28.55],
    "lon":   [77.20,    77.23,    77.17,    77.28,    77.15],
    "value": [142,      168,      95,       210,       87],
})

# Centre the map on the mean location
m = folium.Map(
    location=[sites["lat"].mean(), sites["lon"].mean()],
    zoom_start=11,
    tiles="CartoDB positron",
)

# Add a CircleMarker for each site, scaled by value
for _, row in sites.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row["lat"], row["lon"]],
        radius=row["value"] / 20,          # scale radius to value
        color="steelblue",
        fill=True,
        fill_opacity=0.7,
        tooltip=f"{row['name']}: {row['value']}",
        popup=folium.Popup(
            f"<b>{row['name']}</b><br>Value: {row['value']}",
            max_width=200,
        ),
    ).add_to(m)

m
